sections.title: A Bit of Tomas and Lawry

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: I'm conducting important business here for the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can help with?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Hmm, I suppose I could use some assistance. However, you're not really the person I need for this task.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Hmm, I suppose I could use some assistance. I'm here to investigate some unusual happenings in Port Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you investigating?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Kourend Council?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Although it's called a kingdom, Great Kourend hasn't actually had a king for many years. These days Kourend is a republic and it is ruled by the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The Council consists of four councillors who are elected by the people every four years. The current councillors are Councillor Hughes, Councillor Unkar, Councillor Orson and Councillor Andrews.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well there is something I could use some help with. However, you're not really the person I need for this task.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well there is something I could use some help with. I'm here to investigate some unusual happenings in Port Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you investigating?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Port Piscarilius is home to various gangs. They're a nuisance, but up until now we haven't had much trouble in dealing with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: A new gang calling themselves the Saviours of Kourend has recently formed. That's not surprising as gangs come and go all of the time, but this one is different.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The Saviours of Kourend have been absorbing many of the other gangs. Within a few weeks, they've already become the largest gang in Kourend and more join them each day.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The talk amongst the people is that it's all down to their leader, a mysterious figure known only as the Queen of Thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice name. Why doesn't the ruler of Port Piscarilius do something about this?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Shauna Piscarilius is the ruler of Port Piscarilius, but she spends most of her time galavanting around in that boat of hers rather than looking after her home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. So what do you need me for?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well my original plan was to infiltrate the gang to get close to the queen and capture her. However, I've been unable to make any progress. Apparently, I don't look the part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah not sure what that's all about. You clearly fit right in here.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Quite right. They're quite insistent though, so someone else will need to give it a go. You look like you might be just the type they're after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you trying to say?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: That you look poor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Anyway, if you're up for the task you'll need to infiltrate the Saviours of Kourend, find the Queen of Thieves, apprehend her and then bring her to me.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: So, do you think you can do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Excellent. The Saviours of Kourend must have their headquarters somewhere nearby. You should ask around town and see if anyone knows where they are. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. Until next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. How goes things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: You need to infiltrate the Saviours of Kourend, find the Queen of Thieves, apprehend her and then bring her to me.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The Saviours of Kourend headquarters will be somewhere nearby. You might be able to learn more by asking around town.

sections.title: Talking to the poor looking man/woman

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there. I'm looking for the Saviours of Kourend. Do you know where I can find them?

lines.speaker: Poor looking man/woman
lines.text: Keep your voice down! You've no idea who might be listening!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh sorry. Do you know where I can find them though?

lines.speaker: Poor looking man/woman
lines.text: No I don't! I keep my distance from that lot!

lines.speaker: Poor looking man/woman
lines.text: However, word on the streets is that Robert O'Reilly has been working with them. Perhaps he could help you. He lives in the middle of town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Thanks for the help.

sections.title: Talking to the poor looking man/woman again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Robert O'Reilly?

lines.speaker: Poor looking man/woman
lines.text: He'll probably be at his home. You'll find it in the middle of town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for the Saviours of Kourend and I was hoping you could help me.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Yeah I can help you, but not for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your price?

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: It's been too long since I've eaten some proper food. All we get around here is fish. Bring me a nice stew and I'll help you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I going to get some stew?

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Buy some? Cook some? Doesn't matter to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. I'll return when I have some stew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It just so happens I have some stew right here.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Well that's suspiciously convenient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know right, what are the chances?

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Well hand it over then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Thanks. The Saviours of Kourend make their home in a place called the Warrens. You'll find it in the sewers beneath us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for your help. I'll be on my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: No information for you then. Come back if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Robert again before giving him stew

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Do you have my stew yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: No stew, no information. Come back when you have some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do indeed.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Well hand it over then.

sections.title: Talking to Robert again after giving him stew

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: How goes things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found the Saviours of Kourend yet.

lines.speaker: Robert O'Reilly
lines.text: Well you should look in the sewers beneath us. There you'll find the Warrens, where the Saviours make their home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for the Saviours of Kourend. Do you know where I could find them?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Right here is where you'll find them. What business do you have with the Saviours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping I could join the gang.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Join the gang? Do you think this is a game?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: We don't just let anyone join our gang. If you want in, you'll need to prove your worth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how can I do that?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Since our formation, many of the other gangs in Kourend have joined our cause. However, some still choose to resist.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Our largest rivals are the Vipers and our leader...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Queen of Thieves?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Let me finish!

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Our leader has had enough of the Vipers and wants to make an example of them.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: We need to send a message to the Vipers to let them know that their days are done.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: We'd like you to kill Conrad King, one of their senior members. The man has been a thorn in our side for far too long. Besides, his murderous ways make us look like paragons of virtue in comparison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to kill someone?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in, kill them, kill them?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So like, kill them dead?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Is this going to be a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Good. You'll find Conrad King south of the Piscarilius Foodhall. News travels fast around here so we'll know when the deed is done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I can do it.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Well then we have nothing to discuss. If you change your mind, you'll find Conrad King south of the Piscarilius Foodhall. News travels fast around here so we'll know when the deed is done.

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Conrad King is still alive so I don't know what you're doing here. Go get the job done. He can be found south of the Piscarilius Foodhall.

lines.speaker: Conrad King
lines.text: I'm coming Elizabeth!

lines.speaker: Conrad King
lines.text: I'm coming Elizabeth!

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Word on the streets is that Conrad King has met his maker. The queen is impressed. You should head on in and speak to her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I go in now?

lines.speaker: Devan Rutter
lines.text: Yes! Now get out of my sight!

sections.title: Talking to The Queen of Thieves

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: So you're the one who killed Conrad King. Not bad, but you don't look like much to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'd be surprised.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: No I wouldn't. I've seen hundreds of fools like you and you're all the same. I doubt you'll be sticking around here very long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And neither will you. On behalf of the Kourend Council, I'm bringing you in.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: You truly are a complete and utter idiot.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: Allow me to enlighten you. While you may have been told that the Saviours of Kourend are evil, the truth is quite the opposite. Before we came along, Kourend was filled with violent gangs who cared little for their victims.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess, you're different.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: Shut up and let me speak!

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: Yes the Saviours of Kourend are thieves, the Saviours of Kourend are murderers. However, we do not randomly pick our targets. Everything we do has a purpose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: The name isn't a joke. We're here to save Kourend. To save it from the corruption that has tainted this once great kingdom.

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: We do that by taking from those who would lord themselves over the people. We remove their power and give it back to those who keep this kingdom alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yet you call yourself a queen. Bit ironic don't you think?

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: The irony isn't lost on me. In truth I didn't want to lead at all, but it fell to me because I'm the best at it. Being born a Piscarilius means that you have to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... you're part of the Piscarilius family?

lines.speaker: The Queen of Thieves
lines.text: I am the Piscarilius family. Of course Lady Shauna Piscarilius couldn't well take charge of a gang, so I took on a different name, one that the rich and degenerate are already starting to fear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, you've got my attention. But how do I know you're telling the truth?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: You are clearly fairly new to Kourend, so you might not be fully aware of our current leadership situation. After the last King died, rulership should have passed on to his daughter. However, she was declared mad.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: It was the King's closest advisors that made this declaration. They took over and became the Kourend Council. They've ruled the kingdom ever since.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Now, the councillors are supposedly voted for by the people. However, the seats have been held by the same four people since the start. Pretty suspicious don't you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does sound odd.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Exactly. The Council is clearly corrupt, and we intend to bring that to light. Councillor Hughes is our first target. We have reason to believe that she may be in league with the Disciples of Yama.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Disciples of Yama?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: A cult of Zamorakians who supposedly serve a demon called Yama.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: They've been a problem for a while, but they haven't been deemed dangerous enough to be properly dealt with. Councillor Hughes' influence, no doubt.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Anyway, go to her home and find proof of her corruption. If you find it, you will know I'm telling the truth. If you don't... well don't pretend you care about breaking and entering anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I'll give you benefit of the doubt. But if you're wrong, I'm coming straight back here to apprehend you.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: I'll pretend I didn't hear that. Councillor Hughes' home is on the northern side of Kingstown. Even a fool like you should be able to find it. Don't worry, she's never in.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Once you have the evidence, give it to that Lawry fellow. Provided he's not as useless as you, he'll make sure that she's dealt with.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Now get lost.

sections.title: Interrupting The Queen of Thieves after she reveals who she is

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Oh it's you. Are you going to actually let me finish what I'm saying this time or are you just going to walk off again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you finish this time.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Good. In future you should try not to be so rude.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Anyway, you are clearly fairly new to Kourend, so you might not be fully aware of our current leadership situation. After the last King died, rulership should have passed on to his daughter. However, she was declared

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: mad.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: It was the King's closest advisors that made this declaration. They took over and became the Kourend Council. They've ruled the kingdom ever since.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Now, the councillors are supposedly voted for by the people. However, the seats have been held by the same four people since the start. Pretty suspicious don't you think?

sections.title: Talking to The Queen of Thieves again

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Whatever you want I don't care. Come back to me when you've completed your task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Hello. Thanks again for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. How goes things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't had any luck finding the Queen of Thieves. However, I've come across this letter which you might find interesting.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Blimey! Forget the Queen of Thieves. If this letter is to be believed, Councillor Hughes is working with those vile Disciples of Yama. I must report this to the Council immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So can I have a reward?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: You've done a great service to the people of Kourend. As I'm sure you know, there is no greater reward than the satisfaction of a job well done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Now be on your way. I have a lot of work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know of the Disciples of Yama?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: They're a group of deranged cultists that worship some sort of demon. They're not a big threat compared to the lizardmen, but they're still an enemy to Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Councillor Hughes is helping them? That's not good.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Indeed. I must report this to the Council immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I have a reward?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. Until next time.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: I hear Councillor Hughes has been arrested. Perhaps you're not totally useless after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, you're really not a nice person.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Oh give me a break. You think I care what you think?

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Anyway, I guess this is the part where you expect a reward?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I wouldn't complain.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Fine. As much as I hate to admit it, you've done alright. The arrest of Councillor Hughes can only mean good things for Kourend and her people.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: This is only the beginning though. There's much work to still be done. I may well call on your help again. Or maybe I won't, who knows. Now take these and go.

lines.speaker: Lady Shauna Piscarilius
lines.text: Why are you still here?
